nodes.name: Fort York National Historic Site
nodes.description: Explore Toronto's birthplace featuring restored 1812 military buildings and costumed interpreters.
nodes.premise: POI: Fort York National Historic Site - Explore Toronto's birthplace featuring restored 1812 military buildings and costumed interpreters.

nodes.name: St. Lawrence Market
nodes.description: Historic market since 1803, voted the world's best food market by National Geographic.
nodes.premise: POI: St. Lawrence Market - Historic market since 1803, voted the world's best food market by National Geographic.

nodes.name: Distillery District
nodes.description: Beautifully preserved Victorian industrial architecture, once the largest distillery in the British Empire.

nodes.name: Casa Loma
nodes.description: Gothic Revival castle built in 1914 with secret passages, towers, and elaborate gardens.

nodes.name: Gardiner Museum
nodes.description: Canada's only museum dedicated to ceramic art spanning 4,000 years of history.
nodes.premise: POI: Gardiner Museum - Canada's only museum dedicated to ceramic art spanning 4,000 years of history.

nodes.name: Royal Ontario Museum
nodes.description: World-class museum with 13 million artifacts including dinosaurs and ancient civilizations.

nodes.name: Art Gallery of Ontario
nodes.description: One of North America's largest art museums with Frank Gehry's stunning Galleria Italia.

nodes.name: Graffiti Alley
nodes.description: Kilometer-long outdoor gallery of vibrant street art in the Fashion District.

nodes.name: High Park Cherry Blossom Walk
nodes.description: Toronto's largest public park with 400 acres of trails, gardens, and wildlife.

nodes.name: Humber River Trail Cycling
nodes.description: Scenic cycling along the Humber River through forests and wetlands.

nodes.name: Scarborough Bluffs
nodes.description: Dramatic 90-meter cliffs overlooking Lake Ontario with hiking trails and beaches.

nodes.name: Rouge National Urban Park
nodes.description: Canada's first national urban park with forests, wetlands, and rare wildlife.

nodes.name: Kensington Market Food Tour
nodes.description: Self-guided exploration of Toronto's most eclectic neighborhood with diverse food vendors.

nodes.name: St. Lawrence Market Food Hall

nodes.name: Ontario Science Centre

nodes.name: MaRS Discovery District

nodes.name: Ripley's Aquarium

nodes.name: CN Tower EdgeWalk
nodes.description: World's highest full-circle hands-free walk on the CN Tower's outer ledge at 356m.

nodes.name: TIFF Bell Lightbox

nodes.name: The Rex Hotel Jazz Bar

nodes.name: Toronto Islands Ferry Crossing
